title: gleditsia horrida var. caspica
intro: Gleditsia horrida var. caspica, also known as the Caspian gleditsia or Persian silk tree, is a deciduous tree native to the Caspian region with distinctive thorns and compound leaves. The tree is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Gleditsia horrida var. caspica include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting immune function. In herbal preparations, the tree's bark, leaves, and seeds are often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are believed to promote overall well-being and alleviate symptoms of certain diseases.
